steps.title: Check current dragonfly deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dragonfly version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
helm list -n dragonfly 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dragonfly installation.

steps.title: Review dragonfly configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dragonfly configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get configmap -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dragonfly
```
Hey Folks,

I just stumbled across this insane project - great work so far! 🚀  

I was wondering if you guys are already working on a `helm`-Chart in order to deploy `dragonfly` to Kubernetes?
It would be a lot easier for us to test `dragonfly` in

steps.title: Apply the fix for Helm-Chart
steps.description:
This is a first, working, but kinda rudimentary helm chart. This should address #57.

But it supports
- persistence for /data (if enabled, a Statefulset is deployed)
- mounting extra volumes (for logs, debugging, whatever)
- initContainers (adjusting the pod's ulimit, possibly)
- passing extra
```yaml
extraVolumes:
  - name: test-volume
    emptyDir: {}

extraVolumeMounts:
  - name: test-volume
    mountPath: /test
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly
kubectl get events -n dragonfly --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Helm-Chart" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This is a first, working, but kinda rudimentary helm chart. This should address #57.
codeSnippets:
extraVolumes:
  - name: test-volume
    emptyDir: {}

extraVolumeMounts:
  - name: test-volume
    mountPath: /test

initContainers:
  - name: test
    command:
    - sh
    - -c
    - df -h
    image: busybox

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dragonfly installed or the issue environment reproducible.
